desc: Marks a CentralWarningLogger.WarningLogEntry as acknowledged.
Appends a timestamped comment to a clash row in the newest QA results XML (orchestrated parameters: clashId, comment).
Creates a detail line in the active view between two points specified in millimetres.
usecase: User wants to draw a detail line between two known coordinates. AI needs to add annotation lines to a drafting or detail view.

desc: Creates a filled region in the active view from a set of XY points provided in millimetres.
usecase: User wants to create a hatched or solid region to highlight an area on a plan or section. AI needs to annotate a view with a bounded filled region.

desc: Adds an existing view filter to a view template by name.

desc: Adds an existing view filter to a regular (non-template) view identified by name.
usecase: Turn on a discipline filter on a working floor plan or section for QA checks.

desc: Adds an existing Revision to a target ViewSheet's additional revisions list.

desc: Command: Applies linked model display settings from current view's template to all templates.

desc: Applies a named view template to a target view using SetTemplate.ApplyTemplate.
usecase: User wants to apply a standard template to the current view for consistent display. AI is configuring views as part of automated sheet setup or coordination workflows.

desc: Applies default Revit resolution to warnings that have available auto-fixes.
Batch-captures PNG snapshots for every clash row and re-exports QA XML with updated ClashResult.ImagePath values.
Captures a single PNG for the clash identified by clashId and updates the clashresults export.
Changes the TextNoteType of a target TextNote element.
usecase: User wants to change the font/style of a specific text note. User wants to standardise text note types across a sheet. AI needs to batch-update text formatting after a template change.

desc: Runs a clash/interference check on the current selection or a provided set of element IDs.
usecase: User wants to find clashing MEP elements in the current model. User wants to verify no interferences exist after routing changes. AI needs clash data to suggest resolution strategies.

desc: Clash detection between linked model elements (e.g.
usecase: Structural vs MEP clash check before coordination meeting. Identifying where pipes/ducts clash with structural beams or columns. Generating clash reports for BIM coordination.

desc: Clears all entries from CentralWarningLogger.
Configures display properties on a named view template using ConfigureTemplate.

desc: Creates a 3D section box from user-selected elements or rooms.

desc: Runs CreateMepFilterSet.ForProject for one or more service keys, then CreateMepFilterSet.ApplyToAllTemplates to add filters with coordinated overrides to plan templates.
Creates a ParameterFilterElement (view filter) in the active document.

desc: Command: Creates a floor plan view on a target level with optional scope box and template.

desc: Creates a new Revision in the active document with description, date, issued-by / issued-to fields, and an optional issued flag.

desc: Phase 7 Command: Creates a section view along a selected gridline.
usecase: , , // 2. Added attributes: [Keywords], [SelectionInput], [Output] // 3. Added ONE LINE at top of Execute(): using var tracker = CommandTracker.Track(this); // // That's it! Everything else stays the same. using System; using Autodesk.Revit.Attributes; using Autodesk.Revit.DB; using Autodesk.Revit.DB.Architecture; using Autodesk.Revit.UI; using Autodesk.Revit.UI.Selection; using MEPBridge.Revit.Tracking; using Nice3point.Revit.Toolkit.External; namespace MEPBridge.Revit.SheetSetup.Commands { Phase 7 Command: Creates a section view along a selected gridline. User selects TWO inputs: 1. Gridline (from host or linked model) 2. Room (from host or linked model) Uses grid-room boundary intersection for section bounds. Scale: 1:50 Active document must be open. User must have access to select elements. At least one grid and one room must exist in the model (host or linked). A new ViewSection is created in the document, oriented along the selected gridline. Section bounds are clipped to the room boundary intersection. Section is NOT placed on a sheet (separate command). User wants to create a section view showing MEP services along a specific grid line. User needs to see building cross-section at a particular grid location. User wants section through a room at a grid intersection.

desc: Creates a section view from user-specified start and end points or element endpoints.
usecase: User wants to create a section view through two arbitrary points. User wants to create a section view along a linear element (pipe, duct, wall). User needs a custom section not aligned to any grid.

desc: Creates a new view template.

desc: Executes a paired text-filter clash sweep on the active Document, appends coordination IErrorRule findings, and writes timestamped clashresults XML beside the RVT.

Performs a pairwise bounding-box clash pass across every visible element in Autodesk.Revit.DB.Document.ActiveView, then appends coordination rule results for export.
Clashes the current Revit selection against every other host element with a bounding box, then merges coordination findings from RunErrorCheckingRulesOnModel into the exported clash table.

desc: Transfers all items in selected categories from a source .rvt to the current project.
usecase: User wants to apply firm template standards to a new project.

desc: Transfers specific named items within a category from a source .rvt to the current project.
usecase: User wants to selectively transfer specific filters or templates.

desc: Unpins all host grids that are on open worksets in the active document.
usecase: User wants to unpin all grids before repositioning them. User needs to adjust grid layout and all grids are currently locked. User wants to batch-unpin after receiving a new architectural model.

desc: Sets lifecycle status on a clash row (new, active, reviewed, resolved) via orchestrated parameters clashId and newStatus.
Debug command that visualizes corridor width measurement from doors.
usecase: User wants to debug corridor width measurement logic. User needs to verify door-to-wall distance calculations visually. User wants to classify a room as corridor vs circulation based on width.
